description:
A scatter plot showing the distribution of image acquisitions relative to a central "Master" image in terms of temporal distance and perpendicular baseline (B_n). The X-axis represents "Time [day]", ranging from 0 to 630 days, with major ticks at 70-day intervals (70, 140, 210, 280, 350, 420, 490, 560, 630). The Y-axis represents "B_n [m]", ranging from -150 m to +150 m, with major ticks at 50-metre intervals (+50, +100, +150, -50, -100, -150).

The central "Master" image is located at 350 days on the Time axis and 0 m on the B_n axis. There are 17 "slave" image acquisitions, each represented by a blue dot and connected to the "Master" point by a straight blue line.

The approximate coordinates (Time [day], B_n [m]) of the 17 slave acquisitions are:
(70, +25), (140, +60), (210, +25), (280, +35), (420, +60), (490, +25), (560, +115), (630, +100), (70, -80), (140, -50), (210, -25), (280, -110), (360, -60), (420, -110), (490, -70), (560, -50), (630, -35).

The plot illustrates how slave images are distributed across both positive and negative perpendicular baselines relative to the master, and across a temporal range spanning approximately 280 days before and after the master image (from 70 to 630 days).
